data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `Compile` items inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is created in the `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/` directory, but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target, after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about through item groups.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` inside the target (after file generation) so the compiler picks up the generated source file.
